free_text: Systematics of fast cumulative fission yields
- method and
Z numbers for DATA.
- Pu-239,240 prelim.res.
European Institute for transuranium
elements.
RAPSODIE
An integrated flux of 10.**23 n/cm2 in the fast reactor
The target nuclides were separately deposited on
pure Al foil, dried, rolled up and placed into
stainless steel capsules.
Isotope dilution mass - spectrometry and
gamma spectrometry.
The data were normalized to a fission product
abundance in the sample to 200%
The fission sources were grouped into those belonging
to the same element (Z=const.) and those being
isodiapheric, i. e. constant difference between
neutrons and protons, (N-Z).
For grown - in fission of daughter nuclides whenever
this was included in the experiment.
Tbl.1 from Radiochimica Acta 29(1981)61 and
tables from EUR-6738 (same data + Z - numbers and
stat.error).
WWO.
Minor corrections.
Y.N. Subents 004-011 added.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents. EARR,3,1,1981 - not found.
COMMENT deleted.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
The data were averaged over the results from
3 capsules of Pu-239.
Yields for the masses in the range of 116.8 to 124,
126 to 130, 138 to 142 and 147 were interpolated with
the aid of thermal yields (see REL-REF)
Statistical error
Corrected to mass chain yields.
Y.N.
SD: SF8=SPA -> FST in REACTION code. BIB
updated. DATA updated according to presentation in
the Table. REL-REF added.
SD: SF5=CHN -> CUM in REACTION code.
ELEMENT, ERR-S added to the DATA section.
The data were averaged over the results from
2 capsules of Pu-240.
Yields for the masses in the range of 116.8 to 124,
126 to 130, 138 to 142 and 147 were interpolated with
the aid of predicted yields from  E.W.Sidebotham,
TRG-report 2143 (R)(1972).
Statistical error
WWO.
Corrected to mass chain yields.
